<!-- AUTOGENERATED by scripts/gen-readmes.sh — edit scenarios.tsv -->
# Apache HTTPD (AJP + HTTPS) — root-context

App at /, Apache terminates TLS on https://localhost:9443/ and forwards over AJP. WebSocket upgrades cannot ride AJP and bypass it via a server-scope RewriteRule to ws://. AJP carries the request scheme natively so Tomcat sees request.isSecure()=true.

Proxy URL on `https://localhost:9443`: `/`

## Configuration

See [docker-compose.yml](./docker-compose.yml) for service definitions.

Apache HTTPD config (`vaadin.conf`):

```apache
Listen 443

<VirtualHost *:443>
    ServerName localhost

    SSLEngine on
    SSLCertificateFile      /usr/local/apache2/conf/server.crt
    SSLCertificateKeyFile   /usr/local/apache2/conf/server.key

    # AJP carries the request scheme natively, so Tomcat already sees
    # request.isSecure()=true and request.getScheme()=https. The header below
    # is only meaningful on the WebSocket upgrade path that goes over HTTP.
    RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Proto "https"
    RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Port  "9443"

    # WebSocket cannot ride AJP — upgrade requests bypass it and hit the
    # backend's HTTP connector directly. RewriteRule is at VirtualHost scope
    # because it is unsupported inside <Location>.
    RewriteEngine on
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:Upgrade} websocket [NC]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:Connection} upgrade [NC]
    RewriteRule ^/?(.*) "ws://vaadin:8080/$1" [P,L]

    ProxyPass         "/"      ajp://vaadin:8009/
    # ProxyPass         "/"      ajp://vaadin:8009/ secret=${TOMCAT_AJP_SECRET}
</VirtualHost>
```
